title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Poirot visits Hicks, an outdoor and sports equipment shop on the main road, to investigate the origin of the canvas backpack. Adopting an exotic accent and the persona of a man buying gear for a nephew, Poirot purchases a sturdy canvas backpack identical to the one found in Colin MacNab's room. During the transaction, the shopkeeper notes that many students from a nearby dormitory on Chestnut Street frequent the store.

Following this, Poirot meets Inspector Sharpe at a sandwich shop to discuss the investigation. Sharpe provides several updates regarding the residents: Nigel Chapman was found in possession of three poisons, though Sharpe doubts his guilt regarding Celia Austin due to a lack of motive. Sharpe also reveals that Elizabeth Johnston was a Communist and that a handkerchief stained with green ink was found in Patricia Lane's drawer, suggesting a connection to the incident involving Elizabeth's papers. Furthermore, Sharpe notes that Leonard Bateson's father is a patient at Longveil Asylum, Ahmed Ali possessed sexual books and postcards, and subversive pamphlets were found among Chandra Lal's belongings. While a search for illegal passports yielded nothing, the police did find numerous empty brandy bottles in Mrs. Nikolaitis's private cabinet.

Poirot concludes that the investigation must return to the backpack as the starting point of the events. Meanwhile, Mrs. Nikolaitis is seen ascending the stairs after an argument with Jeronimo and Maria, during which she makes disparaging remarks about Italians.
